title: WIP- vendor containers/{image,storage}
body:
Avoid redundantly pulling the same image (or layer).

Signed-off-by: Valentin Rothberg <rothberg@redhat.com>

comments.body: Do not merge this PR, it's used to increase confidence for the changes to storage and image by letting it pass Buildah's CI.
This PR needs to be updated. We also need to merge https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/1400 first to make it pass.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably 3d74031) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
@vrothberg #1400 Is merged can you rework this one?

> @vrothberg #1400 Is merged can you rework this one?

Sure. Already rebased and pushed.

> Can you remove [WIP]?

Not until the PRs over [image](https://github.com/containers/image/pull/607), [storage](https://github.com/containers/storage/pull/307) and [skopeo](https://github.com/containers/skopeo/pull/619) are merged. This PR is testing the new copy-detection mechanism.

I can open a dedicated PR to vendor in the latest image/storage.
All tests are hitting a timeout. I'll vendor the storage changes first to exclude some potential error sources from image.

@mtrmac @nalind @TomSweeneyRedHat the integration tests don't seem to fire off as the last logs are calling `tests/test_runner.sh` and do not show any log afterward. https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/2763 over at libpod is passing now. I try to wrap my head around it I fail to reproduce.
It ultimately suggests a deadlock but I don't see how.

Good news before the weekend. No deadlock and tests are passing now. I took a shot a `travis_wait` which is famous for suppressing output when a timeout is being hit.

I don't yet fully understand WHY we hit the timeout but removing `travis_wait` let's us pass again. One really annoying thing about `travis_wait` is that it does not print *any* log at the time out which complicates debugging.

I've already checked with @TomSweeneyRedHat who wants a head nod from @nalind to make sure that removing `travis_wait` does not introduce any undesired side-effect.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably fcc12bd) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
